title: hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus
intro: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus, commonly known as the Beach Hibiscus or Sea Hibiscus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions, including high blood pressure, diabetes, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus are attributed to its therapeutic actions, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic activities, which are due to the presence of bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. The bioactive constituents of Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus have been shown to exert antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions.
benefits: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus has many benefits, such as serving as a natural remedy for various health issues, including high blood pressure, diabetes, and even cancer, due to its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other beneficial compounds. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions, thereby improving overall quality of life. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome, making it a valuable resource for maintaining a healthy gut. By incorporating hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and enhanced overall well-being, ultimately leading to a longer and healthier life.
actions: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus has many therapeutic actions, such as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of heart disease and stroke. Additionally, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to protect against cell damage and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, while its antimicrobial actions combat infections and promote wound healing. The plant's flavonoids and anthocyanins also exhibit anti-cancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and reducing the risk of certain types of cancer. By incorporating hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus into their daily lives, individuals can experience improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and enhanced overall well-being.
constituents: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce the risk of chronic diseases and improve overall well-being. The plant also contains phenolic acids, including caffeic acid, ferulic acid, and sinapic acid, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, aiding in the prevention of infections and promoting a healthy immune system. Additionally, the plant's essential oils, including geraniol, linalool, and beta-caryophyllene, have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality. These medicinal constituents can improve people's daily lives by providing natural remedies for various health conditions, allowing individuals to live healthier, happier, and more productive lives.
parts: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, roots, and bark, which are used to treat various ailments. The leaves of Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as saponins and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of this plant are a rich source of mucilages, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The roots and bark of Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus are rich in alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which have been used to treat digestive issues and skin problems.
preparations: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions. The tea made from the leaves and flowers of this plant is used to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The tincture is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, and is also used to reduce inflammation. Additionally, the infusion is used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
side_effects: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its high fibre and tannin content. Excessive consumption of the plant may also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching, in some individuals. Furthermore, using hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. elatus as a treatment for diabetes or blood pressure without proper medical supervision can interact with certain medications and worsen the condition. Additionally, its high caffeine content can cause insomnia, anxiety, and an irregular heartbeat if consumed in large quantities.
